questions.question: What is the result of the unary relational operation 'NOT A' on a relation A?
options: A) All elements in A are removed
B) All elements in A are kept
C) All elements in A are duplicated
D) All elements in A are negated
questions.explanation: The unary NOT operation inverts the set of elements in A, resulting in all elements being removed.

questions.question: What is the result of the binary relational operation 'PROJECT' on relations R and S?
options: A) A relation containing only the common attributes of R and S
B) A relation containing all columns of R and S
C) A relation containing only the columns of S and R with common attributes
D) A relation containing all columns of R and S
questions.explanation: The PROJECT operation returns a relation containing only the common attributes of R and S.

questions.question: What is the result of the aggregate relational operation 'SUM' on relation R?
options: A) A relation containing the sum of each attribute in R
B) A relation containing the count of each attribute in R
C) A relation containing the average of each attribute in R
D) A relation containing the maximum value of each attribute in R
questions.explanation: The SUM operation returns a relation containing the sum of each attribute in R.

questions.question: What is the result of the grouping relational operation 'GROUP BY' on relation R?
options: A) A relation containing the group name and the aggregate value for each group
B) A relation containing all columns of R
C) A relation containing the count of each group
D) A relation containing the sum of each attribute in R
questions.explanation: The GROUP BY operation returns a relation containing the group name and the aggregate value for each group.

questions.question: What is the result of the join relational operation 'INNER JOIN' on relations R and S?
options: A) A relation containing all columns of R and S
B) A relation containing all rows of R and S where the join condition is met
C) A relation containing the union of all rows of R and S
D) A relation containing the intersection of all rows of R and S
questions.explanation: The INNER JOIN operation returns a relation containing only the rows of R and S where the join condition is met.
